# Python Environment & Terminal Setup

Before running any manual python scripts, modules, or tests from the command line, you must activate the virtual environment and ensure your paths are set correctly so that internal imports (`libs.utils`) resolve properly.

```bash
# 1. Define the root of the Git repository
ROOT="/home/evezeyl/Documents/Insync/gdrive/OBSWORK/20_GITS/SPARMVET_VIZ"

# 2. Activate the virtual environment
source $ROOT/.venv/bin/activate

# 3. Add the project root to the PYTHONPATH
export PYTHONPATH=$ROOT:$PYTHONPATH
```

If you plan on running these frequently, it is recommended to add these lines to a local `.bashrc` or an environment `.env` file that is ignored by git.
